claimText: An ex-post facto environmental clearance (EC) allows a project to continue operations even if it did not obtain an EC prior to the commencement of the project.
evidenceExcerpt: An ex-post facto EC allows a project proponent to continue without obtaining an EC prior to project commencement.
examUse: Statement-based question on the definition and implications of ex-post facto EC in environmental governance.
distractorHooks: Ex-post facto EC is granted only before project commencement
Ex-post facto EC strictly prohibits continuation of projects without prior clearance

claimText: The Supreme Court overturned its previous decision that had restrained the Centre from granting ex-post facto environmental clearance to mining projects.
evidenceExcerpt: The Supreme Court reviewed and overturned its May 2025 decision in Vanashakti v. Union of India, which had restrained the Centre from granting ex-post facto environmental clearance (EC) to mining projects
examUse: Statement-based question on recent judicial trends regarding EIA and mining projects.
distractorHooks: The Supreme Court completely banned ex-post facto ECs for mining projects
The Centre is constitutionally barred from granting ex-post facto ECs under all circumstances
